an1.31:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when untamed, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.31:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when untamed, leads to great harm."
an1.32:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when tamed, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.32:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when tamed, leads to great benefit."
an1.33:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when unguarded, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.33:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when unguarded, leads to great harm."
an1.34:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when guarded, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.34:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when guarded, leads to great benefit."
an1.35:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when unprotected, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.35:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when unprotected, leads to great harm."
an1.36:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when protected, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.36:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when protected, leads to great benefit."
an1.37:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when unrestrained, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.37:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when unrestrained, leads to great harm."
an1.38:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when restrained, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.38:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when restrained, leads to great benefit."
an1.39:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.39:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained, leads to great harm."
an1.40:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, that, when tamed, guarded, protected, and restrained, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.40:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when tamed, guarded, protected, and restrained, leads to great benefit."
an1.40:1.4: The Chapter on the Untamed, the fourth.
